section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing
Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents
Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, equipment — not present in Tier 3
Commencing any Phase 8 activity before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed
Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4
Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding its scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic
Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete
Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable
Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible
Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4
Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions
Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional

check_notes.13.1: No references to Grant Agreement Annex, AGA Template, or Model Grant Agreement template found as structural authorities. CC-10 references 'HE RIA/IA Part B structure from application form template' citing docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/application_forms/ria_ia/af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf (Tier 2A application form, not grant annex).
check_notes.13.2: Artifact is an assembled draft index with cross-section consistency checks. It does not assert call requirements, scope boundaries, or eligibility conditions. No call constraint claims are made in the artifact's own content.
check_notes.13.3: All project-specific facts referenced (partner names, MAESTRO, FIIT, OBJ-7, deliverable IDs D1-02 through D9-02, WP1–WP9) appear in consistency checks with source_refs pointing to Tier 3 paths (docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json, docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json). Traceability_footer.primary_sources includes Tier 3 references.
check_notes.13.4: Artifact schema_id 'orch.tier5.part_b_assembled_draft.v1' identifies it as a Phase 8 artifact. Budget gate assessment file (docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json) is outside this skill's declared inputs and could not be read to verify gate passage. Artifact-level scan: CC-08 explicitly confirms 'no specific budget figures, person-month values, or cost allocations appear in any of the three sections; Implementation references budget gate passage but cites no numeric budget data.' No patterns matching €[0-9]+, [0-9]+ EUR, or [0-9]+ person-months detected in artifact content. No budget-dependent claims present. Budget gate passage verification deferred to orchestration-level gate evaluation.
check_notes.13.5: Not directly checkable from artifact content. This prohibition is enforced by the decision-log-update skill at the agent/orchestration level, not at the artifact-audit level.
check_notes.13.6: Not directly checkable from artifact content. This prohibition applies to workflow and skill scope definitions, not to deliverable artifact content.
check_notes.13.7: Artifact references Tier 5 section outputs and Tier 3/2A inputs. No evidence of Phase 8 artifact referencing outputs from phases that should be downstream. All referenced phases (Tiers 2A, 3) are upstream of Phase 8. No silent reordering detected.
check_notes.13.8: Artifact has no_unsupported_claims_declaration: false — honestly declares potential unsupported claims rather than asserting full support. CC-06 explicitly flags 'partial metric loss for OBJ-7 in the Impact section' with status 'inconsistency_flagged'. CC-12 explicitly notes 'inferred' validation status for Excellence and Impact sections and identifies specific claims (CLM-05, CLM-13, CLM-14, ei04-coverage) for downstream review. All gaps and limitations are explicitly flagged in the consistency_log, not silently papered over.
check_notes.13.9: No regulatory claims about Horizon Europe participation conditions, grant agreement terms, or financial regulation are made in this artifact. Structural references cite docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/application_forms/ria_ia/af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf (Tier 2A source document), not generic programme knowledge.
check_notes.13.10: Artifact is Tier 5 (assembled draft). no_unsupported_claims_declaration is false. Artifact does not contain validation_status.claim_statuses[] array. All consistency_log entries have populated source_refs arrays pointing to Tier 3 and Tier 5 paths. Traceability_footer.primary_sources contains 6 entries spanning Tier 2A, Tier 3, and Tier 5 section artifacts. derivation_note explains inheritance of section-level traceability. No unresolved claims without source references found in the artifact's own content.
check_notes.13.11: Not directly checkable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition requires comparing Tier 1/2 source documents to their original state, which is outside the scope of artifact-level audit.
check_notes.13.12: Not directly checkable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition applies to agent and skill execution behavior, not to deliverable artifact content.
